title: aconitum toxicum subsp. toxicum
intro: Aconitum toxicum subsp. toxicum, also known as monk's hood or wolf's bane, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ranunculaceae. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Aconitum toxicum subsp. toxicum has been valued for its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, which have been used to treat a range of health issues, including pain, fever, and respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in tinctures, infusions, and ointments, and its health benefits include reduced inflammation, improved circulation, and enhanced immune function. However, due to its toxicity, Aconitum toxicum subsp. toxicum should be used with extreme caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
